CSVTools.description: Agent for handling CSV files. Pass a single structured query string. Required format: '<operation> <path>' or '<operation> <path> with content: <content>'. Operations: read, write, append, get_headers. Examples: 'read data/sales.csv', 'append data/sales.csv with content: [{"name": "Alice", "score": 90}]', 'get_headers data/sales.csv'. Only .csv files are accepted.

functions.read: Read the contents of a csv file specified by a path. Optionally set n_rows (int) to limit the number of data rows returned; combine with from_end=true to get the last n rows instead of the first. Optionally set columns (list of strings) to return only the specified column headers.
functions.write: Write new content to a csv file specified by a path. Content should be formatted similar to [{"col_a": "val_a"}, {"col_b": "val_b"}] where "col_a" and "col_b" are column names and in the column_names parameter. This tool will overwrite the previous content of the csv file.
functions.append: Append new content to a csv file specified by a path. Content should be formatted similar to [{"col_a": "val_a"}, {"col_b": "val_b"}] where "col_a" and "col_b" are column names and in the column_names parameter.
functions.get_headers: Return the column headers of a csv file specified by a path. Use this before reading or writing to verify exact column names and resolve any spelling ambiguity.

TXTTools.description: Agent for handling text files. Pass a single structured query string. Required format: '<operation> <path>' or '<operation> <path> with content: <content>'. Operations: read, write, append, clear. Examples: 'read notes.txt', 'write report.txt with content: quarterly results here', 'append log.txt with content: new entry', 'clear scratch.txt'. Only .txt files are accepted. Always provide the full path.

functions.read: Read the contents of a txt file specified by a path.
functions.write: Write new content to a txt file specified by a path. This tool will overwrite the previous content of the txt file.
functions.append: Append new content to a txt file specified by a path. This tool will append the new content to a new line after the previous content of the txt file.
functions.clear: Clear the contents of a file specified by a path.

read_args.path: The file path to read from.

write_args.path: The file path to write to.
write_args.content: The content to write to the file.

DirectoryTools.description: Agent for managing directories. Pass a single structured query string. Required format: '<operation> <path>'. Operations: list, create directory. Examples: 'list data/reports', 'create directory data/archive'. Always provide the full directory path.

functions.get_directory_content: Get the contents of a directory specified by a path.
functions.create_directory: Create a new directory specified by a path.

TraversalTools.description: Agent for searching the file system. Pass a single structured query string. Required format: 'find <name> in <directory> using <dfs|bfs> [approximate]'. Use 'approximate' when the name may be misspelled or partially known. Examples: 'find report.txt in . using dfs', 'find logs in data using bfs', 'find reprot.txt in . using dfs approximate'. Always specify the directory (use '.' for current).

functions.depth_first_search: Performs a depth-first search (DFS) through the filesystem. The path(s) to matching file(s) or directories, or an informative message if not found.
functions.breadth_first_search: Performs a breadth-first search (BFS) through the filesystem. The path(s) to matching file(s) or directories, or an informative message if not found.

search_args.source_directory: The directory path from which the search will commence.
search_args.target_name: The name of the file or directory to search for. Defaults to '' if unspecified.
search_args.approximate: A boolean indicating whether to use approximate/fuzzy matching (True) or exact matching (False). Defaults to False.
